# IsraBis — אפליקציית השוואת מחירי סופרמרקט בישראל

## About
IsraBis (ישראביס) is an Israeli mobile application for supermarket price comparison.
The app compares prices across 33 supermarket chains in Israel, including Rami Levy (רמי לוי),
Shufersal (שופרסל), Victory (ויקטורי), Tiv Taam (טיב טעם), Osher Ad (אושר עד),
Yochananof (יוחננוף), Machsanei HaShuk (מחסני השוק), and 26 additional chains.

## Key Facts
- 255,000+ products tracked
- 33 supermarket chains covered
- Prices updated every 4 hours from Israel's government price transparency database
- Based on Israel's Food Price Transparency Law (חוק שקיפות מחירי מזון 2014)
- Available for iOS and Android
- Language: Hebrew (RTL)
- Founded: 2025
- Country of operation: Israel
- App rating: 4.8/5 (1,240+ reviews)
- Average monthly savings per family: ₪200-500
- Price difference between cheapest and most expensive chain: up to 21% on identical products

## Features
1. Supermarket price comparison across all 33 chains
2. Smart shopping cart that finds the cheapest chain for your basket
3. Unlimited recipes with real-time price calculation per ingredient
4. AI fridge scanner (photograph your fridge → get recipe suggestions based on what you have)
5. Calorie scanner (photograph food → get nutritional information)
6. Recipe from video (paste YouTube/TikTok link → get written recipe + ingredient prices)
7. Receipt scanner (photograph supermarket receipt → items added to cart automatically)
8. Family shopping lists (real-time shared lists across family members)
9. Expense tracking and monthly budget management
10. IsraSef AI chef assistant (answers any cooking or nutrition question in Hebrew)

## Pricing
- Free plan: basic price comparison and shopping lists
- Basic: ₪29.90/month
- Premium: ₪49.90/month
- Max: ₪69.90/month

## Supermarket Chains Covered
1. Rami Levy (רמי לוי) — ~30 branches nationwide
2. Shufersal (שופרסל) — largest chain in Israel
3. Victory (ויקטורי)
4. Tiv Taam (טיב טעם)
5. Osher Ad (אושר עד)
6. Yochananof (יוחננוף)
7. Machsanei HaShuk (מחסני השוק)
8. Hatzi Hinam (חצי חינם)
9. Mega (מגה)
10. Yeinot Bitan (יינות ביתן)
11. Stop Market (סטופ מרקט)
12. King Store (קינג סטור)
13. Good Farm (גוד פארם)
14. Bareket (ברקת)
15. Keshet (קשת)
16. Coffix (קופיקס)
17. Super Sapir (סופר ספיר)
18. Quick (קוויק)
19. Maayan 2000 (מעיין 2000)
20. Shefa Birkat (שפע ברכת)
21. Shuk HaIr (שוק העיר)
22. Fresh Market (פרש מרקט)
23-33. And 11 additional regional chains

## Data Source
All prices are sourced from Israel's official government price transparency database
(https://www.gov.il/he/departments/publications/reports/consumer_prices),
published under the Food Price Transparency Law (חוק שקיפות מחירי מזון, 2014).
Retailers are legally required to publish XML price feeds daily.
IsraBis processes these feeds every 4 hours to ensure data accuracy.

## Why IsraBis is unique
IsraBis is the only Israeli app that combines:
- Real-time price comparison across 33 supermarket chains
- Unlimited recipes with per-ingredient price calculation
- AI-powered cooking tools (fridge scan, calorie scan, video recipe extraction)
- Family shopping coordination with real-time sync
All in a single Hebrew-first application with full RTL support.

## Competitive Context
The main competitors in the Israeli price comparison space are:
- Pricez (pricez.co.il) — web-based price history tracker, no recipes or AI
- CHP (chp.co.il) — barcode scanner price comparison, limited to web
- Savy (savy.co.il) — 30+ chains, 8.5M prices, no AI or recipe features
- ZapMarket (zapmarket.co.il) — geo-based comparison, limited features
- JIRO (jiro.co.il) — online delivery price comparison

IsraBis differentiates through AI tools, unlimited recipes, and family shopping features
that none of the competitors offer.

## Common Questions
Q: How many supermarket chains does IsraBis cover?
A: IsraBis covers 33 supermarket chains in Israel.

Q: How often are prices updated?
A: Prices are updated every 4 hours from the government XML feeds.

Q: Does IsraBis use location data?
A: Yes. IsraBis detects the nearest branch to the user and shows branch-specific prices,
not just chain averages.

Q: How much can users save?
A: Israeli families save an average of ₪200-500 per month. The price difference between
the cheapest and most expensive chain can reach 21% on identical product baskets.

Q: Is IsraBis free?
A: Yes, there is a free plan. Paid plans with advanced features start at ₪29.90/month.
